ai_literacy.overview: Complete the self-assessment reflection questions after reading all lessons. There is no hands-on exercise for this module — just share your honest thoughts.
instructions: Read through all 6 lessons in the **Learn** tab to build your understanding of AI for research administration.
When ready, switch to the **Challenge** tab.
Complete the **Self-Assessment** by answering all 3 reflection questions.
Click **Submit Self-Assessment** to save your answers.
Click **Check Progress** to verify completion, then **Complete Module** to earn your 50 XP.

star_criteria.3: Complete all 3 self-assessment reflection questions (everyone earns 3 stars)

process_mapping.overview: Reflect on your team's document processes. There is no hands-on exercise — answer the 4 process reflection questions to complete.
instructions: Read through all 6 lessons in the **Learn** tab to build your process thinking skills.
Think about a specific repetitive task your team does that involves processing documents.
Switch to the **Challenge** tab and complete the **Process Reflection** by answering all 4 questions.
Click **Submit** to save your answers.
Click **Check Progress** to verify completion, then **Complete Module** to earn your 100 XP.

star_criteria.3: Complete all 4 process reflection questions (everyone earns 3 stars)

workflow_design.overview: Reflect on how you would design workflows for your processes. There is no hands-on exercise — answer the 4 design reflection questions to complete.
instructions: Read through all 6 lessons in the **Learn** tab to understand workflow design principles.
Think about how you would translate one of your team's processes into a workflow architecture.
Switch to the **Challenge** tab and complete the **Design Reflection** by answering all 4 questions.
Click **Submit** to save your answers.
Click **Check Progress** to verify completion, then **Complete Module** to earn your 100 XP.

star_criteria.3: Complete all 4 design reflection questions (everyone earns 3 stars)

foundations.overview: Build your first extraction workflow using a sample NSF proposal. Click **Set Up Lab** above to add the PDF to your workspace, then follow the steps to create a workflow, extract data from it, and verify the results. When you click **Check Progress**, the system validates that your workflow extracted the correct values from the document.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample NSF proposal to your workspace.
Go to your **Library** tab and click **New** to create a workflow.
Create a workflow named **NSF Proposal Extractor**.
Add a step with an **Extraction** task.
Create a Extraction with fields: `PI Name`, `Institution`, `Total Budget`, `Project Period`, `Sponsoring Agency`.
Run the workflow against **nsf-proposal-alpine-ecology.pdf**.
Verify: PI Name = Sarah Chen, Total Budget = $485,000.

star_criteria.1: Extract 3+ required fields and run the workflow
star_criteria.3: Extract 8+ fields including Co-PI, Abstract, Program Officer

extraction_engine.overview: Create a comprehensive extraction with 15+ fields using a sample NIH R01 proposal. Click **Set Up Lab** to add the PDF, then build a detailed Extraction and validate your results.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample NIH R01 proposal to your workspace.
Create a new Extraction with 15+ fields covering all sections of the NIH proposal.
Include fields for: PI, institution, grant number, budget categories (personnel, equipment, travel, indirect costs), specific aims, human subjects, vertebrate animals, and key personnel.
Create a workflow with an Extraction task using your comprehensive Extraction.
Run the workflow against **nih-r01-neuroscience.pdf**.
Review the results — verify PI Name = James Park, Total Budget = $1,250,000.

star_criteria.1: Extract 15+ fields from the NIH proposal
star_criteria.2: Extract 20+ fields including all budget categories
star_criteria.3: Extract 25+ fields, using Allowed values to constrain categorical fields and marking situational fields as optional

multi_step.overview: Build a multi-step workflow pipeline using a sample subaward agreement. Click **Set Up Lab** to add the PDF, then create a 3+ step workflow that chains extraction, analysis, and formatting.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample subaward agreement to your workspace.
Create a 3-step workflow:
**Step 1** — Extraction: Create a Extraction with fields for parties, amounts, period, PI, deliverables, and compliance terms.
**Step 2** — Prompt: Write a prompt that analyzes the extracted subaward data and identifies key obligations and deadlines.
**Step 3** — Formatter: Format the analysis into a structured compliance summary report.
Run the workflow against **subaward-agreement.pdf**.
Verify the pipeline chains correctly: extraction → analysis → formatted report.

star_criteria.1: Build a 3-step workflow with Extraction + Prompt + Formatter
star_criteria.2: Build a 4+ step workflow with clear data flow between steps
star_criteria.3: Build a 5+ step workflow using 4+ different task types

advanced_nodes.overview: Use advanced workflow nodes (the Research node) and parallel tasks to process a sample budget justification. Click **Set Up Lab** to add the PDF, then build a workflow with an advanced node and a step that runs two tasks at once.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample budget justification to your workspace.
Create a workflow that processes the budget document using advanced nodes:
**Step 1** — Extraction: Extract the salary table, supply costs, travel, subawards, and indirect costs.

**Step 2** — Research node: Ask it to analyze the extracted figures and check whether the line items add up to the stated total. The Research node makes two LLM passes over the data — no URL or API key needed.
**Step 2 (parallel)** — Add a second task to the same step: a Prompt task that writes a short budget narrative summary. Two tasks in one step run concurrently.
Run the workflow against **budget-justification.pdf**.
Verify the Research node produces a report and that both parallel tasks complete.
Note: the **Code Execution** node (sandboxed Python) is an admin-only power node and is not in the standard palette — the Research node covers this exercise for everyone.

star_criteria.1: Use one advanced node (Research, API, or Crawler) and a step with 2+ parallel tasks
star_criteria.3: Run 3+ parallel tasks within a single step

output_delivery.overview: Create a workflow that produces a downloadable deliverable from a sample progress report. Click **Set Up Lab** to add the PDF, then build a pipeline that extracts, summarizes, and exports the data.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add the sample progress report to your workspace.
Create a workflow that produces a downloadable deliverable from the progress report:
**Step 1** — Extraction: Extract accomplishments, publications, students trained, budget expenditures, and milestones.
**Step 2** — Prompt: Summarize the progress and highlight key achievements.
**Step 3** — Document Renderer or Data Export: Produce a downloadable report or CSV export.
Run the workflow against **progress-report-year2.pdf**.
Download the output file and verify it contains the extracted data.

Project Title: Alpine Ecosystem Response to Climate Change

star_criteria.1: Use a Document Renderer, Data Export, Package Builder, or Form Filler and run the workflow
star_criteria.3: Use a Package Builder to create a ZIP archive with multiple output files

validation_qa.overview: Add quality checks to a workflow you've already built. This module reuses the NSF proposal from Module 1 — no new documents needed.
instructions: This module reuses the NSF proposal from Module 1 (already in your workspace if you completed that module).
Open the workflow you created in Module 1 (or create a new one for this document).
Go to the **Validate** tab in the workflow editor.
Create a validation plan with at least 2 quality checks (e.g., 'PI Name is not null', 'Total Budget is a valid number').
Run validation against the NSF proposal document.
Review the validation results — check which quality checks passed and which failed.
Iterate on your extraction if needed to improve validation scores.

star_criteria.1: Create a validation plan with 2+ checks and run a validated workflow
star_criteria.2: Create a validation plan with 5+ quality checks
star_criteria.3: Create a validation plan with 8+ checks covering completeness, accuracy, and format

batch_processing.overview: Run a workflow across multiple documents at once. Click **Set Up Lab** to add three sample proposals, then process them all in batch mode and verify the results.
instructions: Click **Set Up Lab** to add three sample NSF proposals to your workspace.
Use an existing extraction workflow (from Module 1 or 2) or create a new one.
Select all three batch documents: **proposal-batch-1.pdf**, **proposal-batch-2.pdf**, **proposal-batch-3.pdf**.
Run the workflow in **Batch** mode.
Monitor the batch progress as each document is processed.
Verify all 3 documents completed successfully with correct PI names: Maria Lopez, Robert Kim, Amara Okafor.

star_criteria.1: Run a batch of 3+ documents with all completing successfully

governance.overview: Organize, verify, and share your workflows across spaces. This module uses workflows you've already built — no new documents needed.
instructions: This module uses workflows you've already built — no new documents needed.
If you don't already have multiple spaces, create a second space from the **Spaces** page.
Build or duplicate a workflow in your second space.
Ensure your workflow has a clear description explaining what it does.
Mark the workflow as **verified** in the workflow settings.
Try exporting the workflow as a `.vandalizer.json` file and importing it into another space.
You now have a verified, portable, well-documented workflow.

star_criteria.1: Mark a workflow as verified and use workflows across 2+ spaces
